_README: OPEN SEAM — see docs/PROBE-REGISTRY.md before appending. Append one row per registered probe to the 'probes' list; do NOT edit tools/audit_probe_aging.py logic. The aging engine runs each row's cmd, classifies the outcome as a GROUND touch (verified/DRIFT) or a brownout (INDETERMINATE/unparseable/missing-input/no-date/configured-host-rc127), and records it in .probe-health-baseline.json. A probe with NO ground-touch in N consecutive runs ages to DRIFT.

probes.physics: git rev-parse origin/main vs committed .handoff-sha (git + filesystem)
probes.note: F7: prose-rewrite defeat closed on main (241be73); reads committed .handoff-sha, absence -> DRIFT (a GROUND verdict), only an unreachable origin -> INDETERMINATE.

probes.physics: doc claims reconciled vs the live host via operator ambient SSH (slop-reality-probe on the host)
probes.note: S-75 / LR-2: rides operator ambient SSH. NO host configured + unreachable -> quiet INDETERMINATE (do not cry wolf headless). A CONFIGURED host whose probe returns rc127 (slop-reality-probe not installed) -> DRIFT (should be installed).

probes.physics: status file presence + mtime + parseable State marker on the filesystem
probes.note: report 4g (:200): an unparseable status file -> continue collapses absence-of-ground to the match color. 'OK:' counts as a ground touch (the tool read the run dir); the genuine unparseable-continue leg is S3's source-level fix. Until then the engine cannot distinguish a clean read from an unparseable-skip via output alone — documented limitation.

probes.physics: BACKLOG.md [park] entries parsed for a re-eval date on the filesystem
probes.note: report 4g (:21): the dateless [park] leg skips rather than going red — the no-date brownout. 'OK:' counts as a ground touch (the tool read BACKLOG.md); the dateless-skip leg is S3's source-level fix.

probes.physics: /proc/net/tcp{,6} LISTEN sockets, resolved to SLOP's target port (8080)

wrong_target.note: bound_port==22 (sshd, the lowest LISTEN socket) means the probe emitted a value for the WRONG target — a registry-trackable WRONG-PHYSICS defect, classified as a brownout, NOT a pass. Fixed in slop-reality-probe (_probe_bound_port targets 8080); tracked here so a regression ages red.

probes.physics: filesystem stat of the SLOP triage queue file /home/stack/code/slop/docs/BACKLOG.md
probes.note: BATCH-11 S2: per-ring reachability of the SLOP (repo,file,syntax) triage queue (RING_REGISTRY row 'slop'). Absent -> INDETERMINATE, aged to DRIFT by the engine after N runs.

probes.physics: filesystem stat of the slop-process triage queue file /home/stack/v5/docs/TODO.md
probes.note: BATCH-11 S2: per-ring reachability of the slop-process (repo,file,syntax) triage queue (RING_REGISTRY row 'slop_process'; resolved to docs/TODO.md, same bracket syntax). Absent -> INDETERMINATE.

probes.physics: filesystem stat of the mediastack triage queue file /home/stack/code/mediastack/docs/TODO.md
probes.note: BATCH-11 S2: per-ring reachability of the mediastack (repo,file,syntax) triage queue (RING_REGISTRY row 'mediastack'). NO canonical queue resolved at write-time (work fragmented across docs/TODO_*.md); this probe is INDETERMINATE until a canonical queue is chosen (BACKLOG follow-up). Honest TODO-hole, NOT a silent pass — ages to DRIFT, the intended pressure to resolve it.
